title: Define MAP sync message types
content:
Define the shared `memory:sync` and `skill:sync` message type interfaces that both OpenHive and swarms use.

```typescript
type MapSyncMessage = {
  type: "memory:sync" | "skill:sync";
  resource_id: string;       // syncable_resources ID
  agent_id: string;          // who pushed
  commit_hash: string;       // git commit
  timestamp: string;         // ISO 8601
}
```

This is the contract between swarms and OpenHive. Decide where these types live (shared package, or duplicated in each project).

Implements [[s-78fe]].

feedback.content: Added `MapSyncMessage` interface and `MapSyncMessageType` to `src/map/types.ts`. Added `memory:sync` and `skill:sync` to `WSEventType` in `src/types.ts`. Types live in the OpenHive package alongside other MAP types — swarm implementations will import or duplicate the interface.

title: OpenHive: Subscribe to swarm MAP endpoints for sync messages
content:
When a swarm registers via `POST /map/swarms`, OpenHive should subscribe to the swarm's `map_endpoint` using the declared `map_transport` (websocket / http-sse / ndjson).

OpenHive filters for `memory:sync` and `skill:sync` message types. On receiving either:

1. **Bookkeeping** — update `syncable_resources` sync state:
   - `updateResourceSyncState(resource_id, commit_hash, agent_id)`
   - `createSyncEvent({ resource_id, commit_hash, pusher: agent_id, ... })`
2. **WebSocket broadcast** — emit on `resource:{type}:{id}` channel for web UI / non-MAP clients

This replaces webhooks as the primary sync signal for MAP-aware swarms. Webhooks remain as a parallel path for non-MAP git pushes.

Depends on [[i-5nl8]] for message type definitions.
Implements [[s-78fe]].

feedback.content:
Created `src/map/sync-listener.ts` with:
- `initMapSyncListener()` — connects to all online swarms at startup, subscribes to `mapHubEvents` for new registrations
- `handleSyncMessage()` — validates resource, updates sync state, creates audit event, broadcasts to local WebSocket channels
- WebSocket connection management with exponential backoff reconnection
- Wired into `server.ts` startup/shutdown lifecycle (guarded by `config.mapHub.enabled`)
- Exported from `src/map/index.ts`

title: OpenHive: Relay sync messages to subscribed swarms
content:
After OpenHive receives a `memory:sync` or `skill:sync` message from a swarm, it needs to relay the message to all swarms that are subscribed to that resource.

Flow:
1. Extract `resource_id` from the received message
2. Look up `resource_subscriptions` for that resource
3. Identify which swarms those subscribed agents belong to (agent → swarm mapping via `map_swarms.owner_agent_id` or `map_nodes`)
4. Send the message to each relevant swarm's `map_endpoint` using the swarm's declared `map_transport`
5. Skip sending back to the originating swarm

Depends on [[i-7ahu]] (inbound subscription must work first).
Implements [[s-78fe]].

feedback.content:
Added relay logic to `src/map/sync-listener.ts`:
- `relaySyncMessage()` — looks up resource subscribers, maps subscriber agents to their online swarms via `findSwarmsByOwnerAgentIds()`, sends the message to each swarm's open WebSocket connection (skipping the source swarm)
- Added `findSwarmsByOwnerAgentIds()` to `src/db/dal/map.ts` — queries online swarms owned by a set of agent IDs
- Relay is called from `handleSyncMessage()` after bookkeeping completes

title: Swarm: Emit memory:sync and skill:sync after tool operations

feedback.content:
Created `src/map/sync-client.ts` — a client library for swarm runtimes to participate in MAP sync. Provides:
- `emitMemorySync()` / `emitSkillSync()` — broadcast sync messages to connected clients (including OpenHive's sync listener)
- `handleIncomingConnection()` — register WebSocket clients connecting to the swarm's MAP endpoint
- Exported as `MapSyncClient` from `src/map/index.ts`

Since the swarm runtime (OpenSwarm) is a separate project, this module lives in OpenHive as an importable SDK. Swarm runtimes wire it into their tool lifecycle:
```typescript
const client = new MapSyncClient({ agent_id, hub_ws_url, owned_resources });
client.emitMemorySync({ resource_id: 'res_abc', commit_hash: 'abc123' });
```

title: Swarm: Handle received sync messages and trigger tool pulls
Swarm: Polling fallback for missed sync messages
